name: gradual-token-trend
description: email-classifier input tokens creeping up: 450→520→580 over 3 days. No errors, no latency impact. Could be content shift or prompt regression.

item.title: Gradual input token increase on email-classifier
item.description: Automated alert: email-classifier average input tokens exceeded 550 threshold (3-day trend).
item.note:
Investigated the token trend on email-classifier. Findings:

- Average input tokens per trace (daily):
  - 3 days ago (Jan 12): 452 tokens (48 traces)
  - 2 days ago (Jan 13): 521 tokens (51 traces) — +15.3%
  - Yesterday (Jan 14): 578 tokens (49 traces) — +10.9%
  - Today so far (Jan 15, 12:00 UTC): 594 tokens (32 traces) — +2.8%
- The trend is steady and upward: ~30% increase over 3 days
- Output tokens stable: avg 14 tokens (classification label + confidence) — unchanged
- Latency: p50 490ms → 510ms (within noise, SLO 1000ms)
- Error rate: 0-2% across all 3 days — normal
- Cost impact: minimal so far ($0.74/day → estimated $0.92/day at current rate)

Possible causes investigated:
- Prompt template: checked git log, no changes to email-classifier prompt in last 2 weeks
- Email content: sampled 10 emails from each day — emails from Jan 14-15 appear to have longer bodies on average (marketing campaign season?)
- System prompt: unchanged at 180 tokens
- The increase is in the user message portion (email body), not the system prompt

No errors and no functional impact yet. But if the trend continues linearly, input tokens could reach ~800 in a week, which might start affecting latency and cost more noticeably. Could be seasonal content shift (longer emails) or could indicate that something upstream is sending different email formats.

This warrants tracking but not immediate action.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
